key_insight: Increasing the barrel filling ratio from 43% to 53% produced denser detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings with better wear resistance, despite a slightly higher friction coefficient.
engineering_relevance: Optimizing detonation-spraying fill ratio can improve coating integrity and durability for wear-critical components in harsh mechanical and high-temperature environments.
method: Detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings at two barrel filling ratios: 43% and 53%.
Compared microstructure and phase composition using microscopy and XRD.
Evaluated dry-sliding tribology with ball-on-disc tests at 10 and 15 N and 5 and 10 cm/s.
evidence_signal: The 53% fill coating showed lower porosity and reached a minimum wear rate of 1.23 × 10^-4 mm3/(m·N) under the reported sliding tests.
watch_item: The improved wear resistance came with a higher coefficient of friction, so real-world adoption should consider the friction–wear tradeoff and loading conditions.
paper_title: Effect of Barrel Filling Ratio on the Microstructure, Phase Composition and Tribological Performance of Detonation-Sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr Coatings
